data_sources: Granola (Plymouth Bay Ortho: April 9 meeting notes recovered; all other deals — no Granola data found)

excluded_deals.name: Remix Medical
excluded_deals.reason: Closed out 4/7 — no response since Feb 25 across multiple follow-ups; contact was not the decision maker

excluded_deals.name: Hemorrhoid Centers of America
excluded_deals.reason: Deal broken off by Solv 3/31 — Dr. Chrono EHR incompatibility; no viable integration path

deals.name: GLOC (Great Lakes Orthopaedic Center)
deals.size: Free during pilot → $1.25/resolved call + $0.40/ClearPay usage at scale; 4 locs, 40 physicians + 100 PAs post-acquisition; ~2K calls/week; starting with 2 locations
deals.status_narrative: GLOC signed the agreement on 4/27. Jim (CEO) drove it across the finish line after a multi-week pricing and ROI process. Pricing locked: free during pilot, then $1.25/resolved call + $0.40/ClearPay usage at scale. Katie and Maria finalized the contract as of 4/24; Salesforce update was in progress on 4/27. Implementation kickoff is next. The org recently 3x'd via two acquisitions (14 → 40 physicians, 20 → 100 PAs); rollout starting with 2 locations.
key_risks: Ortho scheduling complexity (age limits, subspecialties) still unproven at scale
No ortho reference customer — Jim flagged this throughout but softened over time
Org just 3x'd via acquisitions; implementation complexity is elevated
deals.next_step: Complete Salesforce update and kick off implementation onboarding. Owner: Brantley. Due: This week.
deals.key_notes_one_line: SIGNED 4/27. Implementation kickoff in progress; Salesforce update pending.

deals.name: CLS Health
deals.size: $3,500/mo (up to 3,000 telemed visits) + 1% SolvPay + $0.50/visit ClearPay; 250 providers total; Texas; 15–30 providers and ~15–30 telemed visits/day to start
deals.champion: Abigail Escalante (EMR Ops Manager, technical lead); Dr. Khaled Attia (clinical champion); CLS President (executive sponsor, actively applying pressure to move)
deals.status_narrative: Deal has two parts as of Teresa's 4/28 update: (1) UC Telemed — green if Solv does not need to read the schedule; (2) Specialties — needs a Kapeesh scoping call. Ideal outcome: one deal covering UC telemed + design partner for specialties. Acceptable fallback: UC telemed only. CLS came back 4/24 with pricing requests framed as wanting to sign: per-encounter or hybrid pricing, 6-month initial term (vs. 1 year), and waived $2K implementation fee. ECW + SolvPay compatibility remains unconfirmed and gates Part 1 entirely. CLS confirmed pVerify cannot handle their auth/referral workflows; Luma also confirmed it cannot do dynamic eligibility — Solv has a clear competitive opening. President is engaged and applying internal pressure. Target launch: June 1.
key_risks: ECW + SolvPay compatibility unconfirmed — gates the entire Part 1 green-light
Pricing concession stack (per-encounter model, 6-month term, waived impl fee) needs finance sign-off and compresses margin
EMR switch to Epic/Oracle possible in ~1 year — CLS wants short-term contract flexibility
deals.next_step: Confirm whether Solv needs to read the schedule for ECW telemed (Owner: Teresa, ASAP — gates Part 1). Schedule Kapeesh call for specialties/design partner scoping. Respond to pricing counter-requests (Owner: Katie/Teresa, this week).
deals.key_notes_one_line: Two-part deal (UC telemed + specialties design partner); ECW+SolvPay gate unresolved; pricing concessions open.

deals.name: Plymouth Bay Orthopedic Associates
deals.size: 1 location active (standalone ortho urgent care, opening June 1); 3 additional ortho clinics as expansion potential; MRR TBD
deals.locations: 1 active (urgent care focus); 3 additional ortho clinics in scope for expansion
deals.champion: Christopher LaFave (Executive Director, ~1yr tenure, has decision authority); Jennifer Hazelton (Dir. of Marketing, 4mo tenure, ex-PhysicianOne, former Solv user — internally tasked to evaluate Solv)
deals.status_narrative: Strong 4/8 team demo with four stakeholders (Executive Director, Ops Manager, Clinical Manager, Dir. of Marketing). Granola notes from the 4/9 follow-up meeting confirm: no major UX or workflow objections; walk-ins, same-day booking, and queue management all landed well for urgent care. ECW real-time sync (2–10 sec) was demonstrated. Critical issue surfaced: HMO patients cannot be seen without a referral for specialty visits — current process is scheduling 2 weeks out then chasing referrals via ECW action items. Solv does not currently handle this referral workflow. Team expected to reconnect ~April 16–17 after internal review; channel has been quiet since 4/12 and that reconnect window is ~2 weeks overdue. ECW live integration demo (data actually flowing into ECW) is the explicit gate before Christopher will commit. June 1 clinic opening is the forcing function.
key_risks: Gone quiet 3+ weeks post-demo — reconnect overdue since week of 4/16
ECW live integration demo is a hard gate Christopher explicitly set before committing
HMO referral workflow gap (Granola-confirmed) blocks expansion to all 3 other ortho clinics
deals.next_step: Brantley to send ECW integration packet + deck (overdue since week of 4/14) and re-engage immediately to schedule reconnect/pricing call before June 1 window closes. Owner: Brantley. Due: ASAP.
deals.key_notes_one_line: 3+ weeks quiet post-demo; ECW live integration is hard gate; June 1 forcing function; HMO referral gap confirmed in Granola notes.

deals.name: Premier Ortho
deals.deal_phase: Clear Pain & Criteria (Formally Paused — was near Paper Process)
deals.size: 6 locations, 17 providers, ~20–25K calls/month; Athena; MRR TBD
deals.champion: Jon Borja (COO), transparent and self-advocating — proactively communicated the Assort blocker rather than going dark
deals.status_narrative: Formally paused since 3/11. Jon (COO) proactively communicated that Assort Health contractual obligations block all movement — 'our hands are tied at the moment' — with no timeline given. This deal was previously at near-Paper Process stage (DocuSign was ready to send) before the Assort blocker surfaced 2/25. Jon confirmed intent to return to Solv when clear. Brantley is on a passive check-in cadence (~every 2 weeks); last targeted check-in was ~4/14, now over 2 weeks overdue as of 4/30.
key_risks: Assort contract exit has no timeline — deal fully stalled with no active path forward
Prolonged pause erodes urgency and creates competitive exposure; check-in now 2+ weeks overdue
If Assort resolves and another vendor has stayed active, deal is at risk
deals.next_step: Brantley to check in now — targeted 4/14 check-in is over 2 weeks late. Passive monitoring only. Owner: Brantley. Due: Today.
deals.key_notes_one_line: Assort contract hard block since 3/11; Jon intends to return when clear; check-in 2+ weeks overdue.

deals.name: Total Primary Care
deals.deal_phase: Clear Pain & Criteria (Cut Off — acquisition-only, 6–9 month hold)
deals.size: 47 locations confirmed; ECW; CTO-built scheduler will not be replaced; acquisition-layer scope only; MRR TBD
deals.champion: Rikin Shah (Chief of Staff); no confirmed executive sponsor
deals.status_narrative: Effectively cut off as of 4/7. TPC's CTO-built scheduler is locked in — 'too many resources poured into it' — and will not be replaced. Rikin wants Solv as a patient acquisition layer only (GMB routing to Solv booking running in parallel with their existing scheduler) with ECW sync for new bookings only. Not on their timeline for 6–9 months. Also actively piloting an unnamed competing voice agent (Rikin declined to name it). Non-standard Monarch deal structure — closer to a Primary Care Connect arrangement. Team accepted the shutdown offer.
key_risks: 6–9 month self-stated hold with zero commitment; long, low-probability path
Acquisition-layer-only scope is non-standard — Solv product appetite for this model is undefined
Competing voice agent pilot already underway with unknown outcome
deals.next_step: Internal Solv decision needed: park for Q3 2026 re-engagement or formally close out. If pursuing, validate acquisition-layer + ECW integration feasibility first. Owner: Teresa/Kapeesh. Due: Now.
deals.next_step_timing: Decision needed now; re-engage Q3 2026 if parked
deals.key_notes_one_line: Cut off 4/7; won't replace scheduler; acquisition-only scope; team must decide to park or formally close.

deals.name: Elevate Health Group
deals.deal_type: Expansion (inferred — Dr. Tovmassian also owns existing Solv partner Mederi Urgent Care)
deals.size: 3 locations, 18 providers; primary care; Athena; SoCal; ClearPay only
deals.champion: Katia Sarafian (Administrator, bought in); Dr. Tovmassian (owner) has explicit product-fit objection
deals.status_narrative: Near-dead. Dr. Tovmassian (owner) pushed back explicitly on 3/16: 'This doesn't make sense for our practice. 90% of our patients are recurring and their insurances are stable.' ClearPay's point-of-care estimate model does not fit a high-recurring primary care patient mix. No response to follow-up requests for a leadership call since 3/24 — dark for 5+ weeks. Real collection pain exists (post-visit statements, IPA routing errors causing billing issues) but the product-fit gap is genuine and owner-level. Team is not pushing hard given Dr. Tovmassian also owns existing Solv partner Mederi Urgent Care.
key_risks: Product-fit objection is explicit and owner-level — not a pricing or timing issue
Dark since mid-March; ghosting all follow-ups for 6+ weeks
Aggressive pursuit risks straining the existing Mederi Urgent Care partnership
deals.next_step: One final low-touch follow-up only. Do not push. Re-engage if ClearPay model evolves to better fit recurring primary care. Owner: Brantley. No active pursuit.
deals.next_step_timing: No active pursuit; potential re-engagement if ClearPay model evolves
deals.key_notes_one_line: Owner's explicit product-fit objection; near-dead since mid-March; pushing risks the Mederi UC partnership.

early_stage_watch.name: OS1 Orthopedic & Sports Injury Clinics
early_stage_watch.stage: Prospecting — post-UCA intro; internal OS1 meeting happened ~4/29; reconnect imminent
early_stage_watch.contact: Randy Johansen (Board Member, former CEO of AFC)
early_stage_watch.notes: Intro via Heather at UCA happy hour. First substantive call 4/24 with Brantley. 4 locations, urgent ortho model. Cash-constrained: opened 3 locations at once; Atlanta/Kennesaw location struggling badly (no MRI for months, hurt volume and revenue). Randy was meeting with OS1 team mid-week (~4/29–4/30) to assess appetite for reviewing solutions. Reconnect with Brantley expected this week (around 5/1). Evan Cory helping prepare Maya and ClearPay collateral. Explicit flag: cash-strapped — could kill the deal before it starts.

executive_summaries.gloc:
**GLOC (Great Lakes Orthopaedic Center)** — Closed Won | Design Partner
- **Size**: Free pilot → $1.25/resolved call + $0.40/ClearPay; 4 locs, 40 physicians + 100 PAs; ~2K calls/week
- **EHR**: Athena
- **Champion**: Jim (CEO)
- **Status**: GLOC signed the agreement on 4/27. Jim drove it across the finish line after a multi-week pricing and ROI process. Pricing locked: free pilot then usage-based at scale. Katie/Maria finalized the contract; Salesforce update was in progress at signing. Implementation kickoff is next.
- **Key risks**:
  - Ortho scheduling complexity (age limits, subspecialties) unproven at scale
  - No ortho reference customer — Jim raised this throughout
  - Org just 3x'd via acquisitions; implementation complexity is elevated
- **Next step**: Complete Salesforce update and begin implementation onboarding. Owner: Brantley. Due: This week.
executive_summaries.cls_health:
**CLS Health** — Paper Process | New Logo + Design Partner
- **Size**: $3,500/mo (up to 3,000 telemed visits) + 1% SolvPay + $0.50/ClearPay; 250 providers total; 15–30 providers/visits per day to start
- **EHR**: ECW
- **Champion**: Abigail Escalante (EMR/technical lead); Dr. Khaled Attia (clinical); CLS President engaged and applying pressure
- **Status**: Teresa clarified deal structure 4/28: Part 1 is UC Telemed (green if Solv doesn't need to read the schedule), Part 2 is Specialties design partner (needs Kapeesh scoping call). CLS came back 4/24 with pricing asks: per-encounter or hybrid pricing, 6-month first term, waived $2K implementation fee. ECW+SolvPay compatibility unconfirmed and gates Part 1. pVerify and Luma both confirmed they can't solve CLS's auth/referral need — Solv has a clear competitive opening. June 1 target.
- **Key risks**:
  - ECW + SolvPay compatibility unconfirmed — gates Part 1 green-light entirely
  - Pricing concession stack needs finance sign-off; compresses margin
  - EMR switch to Epic/Oracle possible in ~1 year; CLS wants short-term contract flexibility
- **Next step**: Confirm whether Solv needs to read schedule for ECW telemed (Owner: Teresa, ASAP). Schedule Kapeesh specialties/design partner call. Respond to pricing counter-requests (Owner: Katie/Teresa, EOW).
executive_summaries.plymouth_bay_ortho:
**Plymouth Bay Orthopedic Associates** — Building Our Champion | New Logo
- **Size**: 1 location (ortho urgent care, June 1 opening); 3 additional ortho clinics as expansion potential; MRR TBD
- **EHR**: ECW
- **Champion**: Christopher LaFave (Executive Director, decision authority); Jennifer Hazelton (Dir. of Marketing, former Solv user, internally tasked to evaluate)
- **Status**: Strong 4/8 team demo — four stakeholders, no major objections, urgent care use case is a clean fit. Granola notes (4/9) confirm ECW real-time sync landed well but HMO referral workflow is a confirmed blocker for specialty expansion: HMO patients cannot be seen without a referral, and Solv does not currently handle this. Team was supposed to reconnect ~4/16–17 after internal review but has gone quiet; that window is 3+ weeks overdue. ECW live integration demo is Christopher's explicit hard gate before committing. ECW integration packet was due week of 4/14 and is overdue.
- **Key risks**:
  - Gone quiet 3+ weeks post-demo; follow-up overdue and forcing function (June 1) is approaching
  - ECW live integration demo is a hard gate before they'll commit
  - HMO referral workflow gap (Granola-confirmed) blocks expansion to all 3 other ortho clinics
- **Next step**: Brantley to send ECW integration packet and re-engage immediately to schedule reconnect/pricing call. Owner: Brantley. Due: ASAP (3 weeks overdue).
executive_summaries.premier_ortho:
**Premier Ortho** — Clear Pain & Criteria (Formally Paused) | New Logo
- **Size**: 6 locations, 17 providers, ~20–25K calls/month; MRR TBD
- **EHR**: Athena
- **Champion**: Jon Borja (COO), transparent and self-advocating
- **Status**: Formally paused since 3/11. Jon proactively communicated that Assort Health contractual obligations block all movement — no timeline given. Was at near-Paper Process before this surfaced. Jon confirmed intent to return to Solv when clear. Brantley's ~every-2-week check-in cadence targeted 4/14 and is now 2+ weeks overdue as of 4/30.
- **Key risks**:
  - Assort contract exit has no timeline — deal fully stalled with no active path
  - Prolonged pause erodes urgency and creates competitive exposure
  - Check-in over 2 weeks overdue; if Assort resolves and another vendor is active, deal is at risk
- **Next step**: Brantley to check in today (2+ weeks overdue from ~4/14). Passive monitoring only. Owner: Brantley.
executive_summaries.total_primary_care:
**Total Primary Care** — Clear Pain & Criteria (Cut Off) | New Logo
- **Size**: 47 locations; ECW; acquisition-layer scope only; MRR TBD
- **EHR**: ECW
- **Champion**: Rikin Shah (Chief of Staff); no confirmed executive sponsor
- **Status**: Effectively cut off as of 4/7. TPC's CTO-built scheduler is locked in and will not be replaced. Rikin wants Solv as acquisition layer only (GMB → Solv booking in parallel), not on timeline for 6–9 months. Actively piloting an unnamed competing voice agent. Non-standard Monarch deal structure; team accepted shutdown offer.
- **Key risks**:
  - 6–9 month timeline with zero commitment; long, low-probability hold
  - Acquisition-layer-only scope is non-standard — Solv product appetite undefined
  - Competing voice agent pilot underway with no visibility into outcome
- **Next step**: Internal Solv decision — park for Q3 2026 re-engagement or formally close out. Owner: Teresa/Kapeesh. Due: Now.
executive_summaries.elevate_health_group:
**Elevate Health Group** — Prospecting (Near Dead) | Expansion
- **Size**: 3 locations, 18 providers; primary care; Athena; SoCal; ClearPay only
- **EHR**: Athena
- **Champion**: Katia Sarafian (Administrator, bought in); Dr. Tovmassian (owner) has explicit objection
- **Status**: Near-dead. Dr. Tovmassian pushed back explicitly on 3/16 — '90% of patients are recurring, insurances are stable, doesn't make sense.' ClearPay doesn't fit high-recurring primary care. Dark since mid-March; no response to follow-ups in 6+ weeks. Real pain exists (post-visit collections, IPA entry errors) but product fit doesn't. Pushing risks the existing Mederi Urgent Care partnership.
- **Key risks**:
  - Product-fit objection is owner-level and substantive — not pricing or timing
  - Dark since mid-March; 6+ weeks of ghosting
  - Aggressive pursuit risks the Mederi Urgent Care relationship
- **Next step**: One final light-touch follow-up only. No active pursuit. Owner: Brantley.
